scenario.name: boots_kart_and_floor
scenario.description: kart sprite (OAM slot 0) + multi-color Mode-7 perspective floor render at standstill (B.6 row 4 boot)

scenario.name: accelerate_lights_speed_bar
scenario.description: holding B builds speed: the speed-bar tick lights (dim tile 0x0A -> lit tile 0x08) in OAM (B.6 row 4 hold b)

scenario.name: steer_rotates_floor
scenario.description: steering LEFT vs RIGHT from one shared baseline rotates the Mode-7 floor differently. axis_branch reloads and re-applies the same prelude per branch (palette phase + forward distance match; the only difference between the endpoint screenshots is the steer direction). The racer floor ANIMATES every frame independently of steering (palette cycle, day-night blend, forward coast-scroll), so a raw capture carried a 1-frame presentation-lag render bucket that made the same-direction control read a large spurious diff intermittently (the S3 remediation paces the perspective rebuild across two frames, which widened the bucket past control_eps until the retry could no longer land both captures in the same phase). The capture now FREEZES the floor first: freeze_button taps START so the palette cycle + day-night clock + camera all stop (R_PAUSE=$5E, read back to prove the freeze took) — a true freeze-frame that renders identically on every run while the steered heading (the thing under test) is held. Each direction still captures a SAME-DIRECTION control run: diff(L,R) is trusted only when diff(L,L') and diff(R,R') are both <= control_eps (now deterministically ~0 under the freeze), a confounded control re-drives that direction (up to max_retries, fail-loud), and the screenshot_axis_diff control_axes gate is the in-assert backstop. Calibration (frozen): good ROMs read ~95% floor diff between LEFT and RIGHT, same-direction controls ~0%, so min_frac 0.15 has a wide margin. Design record + validation data: docs/audit/racer-oracle-steer-gate.md
